description_long: This object is a vintage portable television set, predominantly turquoise in color, with a boxy, plastic body and a convex glass screen on the front. The front panel features a Cyrillic label, a large viewing screen, and a vertical row of physical control sliders and buttons. On top, there is a prominent metal antenna with a rectangular loop, attached to a black base, and a visible power cord extending from the back. The left side displays handwritten Cyrillic text and a taped-on paper note, while the right side is adorned with two pin-up style stickers. The back panel includes ventilation grilles, a rotary knob, and a recessed compartment, likely for batteries or service access. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible seams, molded plastic details, and a retro aesthetic typical of Soviet-era consumer electronics.
description: A retro turquoise portable television with a convex glass screen, metal antenna, and physical control sliders, featuring Cyrillic labels and decorative stickers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, Cyrillic label, and vertical control sliders.
description_view_1: Left side view with handwritten Cyrillic text and a taped paper note, plus the antenna base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the ventilation grilles, rotary knob, power cord, and battery compartment.
description_view_3: Right side view featuring two pin-up style stickers and the top control panel.
